steps.title: Understand margin of error
steps.content:
When we estimate a population mean from sample data, the margin of error tells us how far the estimate might be from the true value. The confidence interval (range of plausible values) extends from:

Lower bound = Sample mean − Margin of error
Upper bound = Sample mean + Margin of error

steps.title: Calculate the confidence interval boundaries
steps.content:
Using the given information:
  • Sample mean = 28.88 ounces
  • Margin of error = 0.13 ounce
Lower bound: $28.88 - 0.13 = 28.75$ ounces
Upper bound: $28.88 + 0.13 = 29.01$ ounces

The plausible values for the average weight of all bags range from 28.75 to 29.01 ounces. That matches choice A — A is correct.
